nodes.label: Hexagonal modular architecture
nodes.rationale: Use-case modules depend on internal ports so provider SDKs, CLIs, tunnels, and transports remain replaceable.

nodes.label: Backend ↔ Local Agent boundary
nodes.rationale: Every local side effect must go through explicit commands and persisted events.

nodes.label: Agent harness
nodes.rationale: A common harness interface avoids coupling use cases to a single CLI.

nodes.label: PersistenceModule
nodes.rationale: Prisma types stay out of controllers and public API contracts; use cases depend on repository ports.

nodes.label: ToolReadinessModule
nodes.rationale: Developer readiness blocks broken sessions, while PM readiness deliberately excludes local tools.

nodes.label: Session start policy
nodes.rationale: PM-started sessions require project membership, an online owning agent, passing developer readiness, and project policy permission.

nodes.label: Session state machine
nodes.rationale: Explicit transitions make orchestration predictable and invalid event ordering rejectable.

nodes.label: Responsive preview presets
nodes.rationale: Viewport changes are client-side only and require no backend mutation.

nodes.label: Validation before review request
nodes.rationale: A draft review request is allowed only after build, tests, lint, and preview pass in AWAITING_PM_VALIDATION.

nodes.label: Review request creation workflow
nodes.rationale: The agent owns local Git operations while the backend keeps the controlled and auditable source-control identity.

nodes.label: Security and permissions
nodes.rationale: PMs never receive shell access or local secrets; commands are protocol-limited and logs/diffs are redacted.

nodes.label: Prisma persistence boundary
nodes.rationale: Prisma Client is isolated behind repository adapters, and atomic multi-entity changes use transactions.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions CI
nodes.rationale: Repository CI is the quality gate for the PairDock codebase, not a PairDock product feature.

nodes.label: Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui styling foundation
nodes.rationale: A single app-owned styling/component foundation avoids competing frameworks and premature abstractions.
